cards.title: Valmiki
cards.subtitle: The First Poet
cards.description: The sage revered as the Adi Kavi, the 'first poet', who composed the Ramayana in Sanskrit. He sheltered the exiled Sita in his hermitage and taught the epic to her twin sons.

cards.title: The Ramayana
cards.subtitle: Rama's Journey
cards.description: One of the two great Sanskrit epics of ancient India, told in seven kandas. It narrates the life of Rama as a model of dharma, devotion, and ideal kingship.

cards.title: Dasharatha
cards.subtitle: King of Kosala
cards.description: The aged ruler of Ayodhya and father of Rama. Bound by a promise of two boons to Queen Kaikeyi, he was forced to exile his beloved son and died of grief soon after.

cards.title: Kaushalya
cards.subtitle: Rama's Mother
cards.description: The eldest queen of Dasharatha and mother of Rama. Gentle and devout, she bore her son's exile with dignified sorrow.

cards.title: Kaikeyi
cards.subtitle: The Queen Who Asked
cards.description: Dasharatha's second queen and mother of Bharata. Swayed by her maid Manthara, she demanded the two boons that sent Rama into exile and Bharata to the throne.

cards.title: Sumitra
cards.subtitle: Mother of the Twins
cards.description: The third queen of Dasharatha and mother of the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna, who served their elder brothers with unwavering loyalty.

cards.title: The Putrakameshti
cards.subtitle: A Sacrifice for Sons
cards.description: Childless, Dasharatha performed a great fire sacrifice for heirs. A divine being rose from the flames with sacred payasam for his queens, and four sons were born.

cards.title: Rama
cards.subtitle: The Seventh Avatar
cards.description: The eldest son of Dasharatha and hero of the epic, an incarnation of Vishnu born to destroy the demon-king Ravana. He is upheld as the ideal of dharma.

cards.title: Bharata
cards.subtitle: The Reluctant Regent
cards.description: Son of Kaikeyi, who refused the throne won for him by his mother's scheme. He ruled Ayodhya only as Rama's steward, placing his brother's sandals upon the throne.

cards.title: Lakshmana
cards.subtitle: Rama's Shadow
cards.description: The devoted twin who followed Rama into exile, serving him and Sita tirelessly. Fierce in battle, he slew the rakshasa prince Indrajit.

cards.title: Shatrughna
cards.subtitle: The Vanquisher of Foes
cards.description: The youngest brother, twin of Lakshmana and constant companion of Bharata. He later slew the demon Lavanasura and founded the city of Mathura.

cards.title: Vishvamitra
cards.subtitle: The Warrior-Sage
cards.description: A king who became a brahmarshi through severe penance. He took the young Rama and Lakshmana to guard his sacrifice and taught them celestial weapons.

cards.title: Vasishtha
cards.subtitle: The Royal Guru
cards.description: The family preceptor of the house of Ikshvaku and guide to its kings. He counselled Dasharatha and conducted the rites of the dynasty.

cards.title: Tataka
cards.subtitle: The Forest Demoness
cards.description: A fearsome yakshini turned rakshasi who ravaged the woodlands and the sages within. Rama slew her at Vishvamitra's bidding, his first great deed.

cards.title: Subduing Tataka
cards.subtitle: Rama's First Battle
cards.description: At Vishvamitra's command the young Rama loosed his arrows against the demoness Tataka, freeing the forest hermitages from her terror.

cards.title: Ahalya
cards.subtitle: The Stone Set Free
cards.description: The wife of the sage Gautama, cursed to lie as stone for an act of deceit by Indra. The dust of Rama's feet released her and restored her form.

cards.title: Liberation of Ahalya
cards.subtitle: The Curse Lifted
cards.description: On the road to Mithila, Rama's touch broke the long curse upon Ahalya, returning her from stone to living woman — a sign of his redeeming grace.

cards.title: Mithila
cards.subtitle: The City of Janaka
cards.description: The capital of the Videha kingdom, ruled by the philosopher-king Janaka. Here Sita was raised and her swayamvara held.

cards.title: Janaka
cards.subtitle: The Philosopher King
cards.description: The wise and detached ruler of Mithila and father of Sita, whom he found in a furrow while ploughing. He set the breaking of Shiva's bow as her bride-price.

cards.title: Sita
cards.subtitle: Daughter of the Earth
cards.description: The daughter of King Janaka, born of the earth and an incarnation of Lakshmi. Her devotion, purity, and endurance are the moral heart of the epic.

cards.title: Pinaka
cards.subtitle: Shiva's Mighty Bow
cards.description: The colossal bow of Shiva kept in Janaka's court, which no king could even lift. Whoever could string it would win the hand of Sita.

cards.title: Breaking the Bow
cards.subtitle: Sita's Swayamvara
cards.description: At the swayamvara Rama lifted Shiva's bow and, drawing it to string it, snapped it in two — winning Sita and announcing his more-than-mortal strength.

cards.title: Marriage of Rama and Sita
cards.subtitle: A Sacred Union
cards.description: With the bow broken, Rama wed Sita at Mithila, and his three brothers married her sister and cousins in a single grand ceremony.

cards.title: Urmila
cards.subtitle: Lakshmana's Wife
cards.description: The daughter of Janaka who married Lakshmana. She remained in Ayodhya through the long exile, her sacrifice of separation often overlooked.

cards.title: Parashurama
cards.subtitle: The Axe-Wielding Sage
cards.description: The fierce brahmin-warrior avatar of Vishnu who confronted Rama after the bow broke. Rama drew Parashurama's own great bow, and the elder yielded to the new age.

cards.title: Manthara
cards.subtitle: The Whispering Maid
cards.description: The hunchbacked servant of Kaikeyi whose poisonous counsel turned the queen against Rama, setting the tragedy of the exile in motion.

cards.title: Kaikeyi's Two Boons
cards.subtitle: A Promise Recalled
cards.description: Reminding Dasharatha of two boons he had once granted, Kaikeyi demanded the crown for Bharata and fourteen years of forest exile for Rama.

cards.title: Vanavasa
cards.subtitle: The Fourteen-Year Exile
cards.description: The long banishment to the forest that Rama accepted without complaint to keep his father's word — the test of dharma at the epic's core.

cards.title: The Exile of Rama
cards.subtitle: Into the Forest
cards.description: Rather than let his father break a vow, Rama left Ayodhya for the wilderness, with Sita and Lakshmana choosing to share his hardship.

cards.title: Death of Dasharatha
cards.subtitle: A Father's Grief
cards.description: Unable to bear the loss of Rama, the old king died calling his son's name, the curse of a bereaved father at last come due.

cards.title: Sumantra
cards.subtitle: The Faithful Charioteer
cards.description: The trusted minister and charioteer of Dasharatha who drove Rama to the forest's edge and returned, heartbroken, to a grieving Ayodhya.

cards.title: Guha
cards.subtitle: The Boatman Chief
cards.description: The Nishada chieftain and ferryman who, in love and reverence, carried Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana across the Ganga on their way to exile.

cards.title: Chitrakoot
cards.subtitle: The Sheltering Hills
cards.description: The forested hill where Rama settled in early exile and where Bharata came to plead for his return.

cards.title: The Paduka
cards.subtitle: Rama's Sandals
cards.description: The wooden sandals Rama gave to Bharata in place of himself. Bharata enthroned them and ruled Ayodhya in their name until Rama's return.

cards.title: Bharata's Plea
cards.subtitle: The Sandals on the Throne
cards.description: Bharata journeyed to Chitrakoot begging Rama to take back the crown. Rama refused for duty's sake, and Bharata bore home only the royal sandals.

cards.title: Dharma
cards.subtitle: The Path of Righteousness
cards.description: The moral law that every figure in the epic must uphold against desire and grief. Rama's unfailing adherence to it is the spine of the story.

cards.title: Maryada Purushottam
cards.subtitle: The Perfect Man
cards.description: The title given to Rama as the ideal man who never transgressed the bounds of virtue, duty, and honour.

cards.title: Dandaka Forest
cards.subtitle: The Vast Wilderness
cards.description: The great southern forest of exile, haunted by rakshasas and dotted with the hermitages of sages whom Rama vowed to protect.

cards.title: Agastya
cards.subtitle: The Sage of the South
cards.description: The venerable sage who received Rama in the Dandaka forest and gifted him divine weapons, including the bow and quiver of Vishnu.

cards.title: Panchavati
cards.subtitle: The Forest Home
cards.description: The grove on the Godavari where Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana built their hut and lived in peace — until Shurpanakha came.

cards.title: Jatayu
cards.subtitle: The Noble Vulture
cards.description: The aged king of vultures and friend of Dasharatha. He gave his life trying to rescue Sita from Ravana's flying chariot.

cards.title: Shurpanakha
cards.subtitle: Ravana's Sister
cards.description: The rakshasi whose advances Rama and Lakshmana spurned, and whom Lakshmana disfigured. Her grievance brought Ravana's wrath upon them.

cards.title: Khara
cards.subtitle: The Demon of Janasthana
cards.description: A rakshasa cousin of Ravana who led an army of fourteen thousand against Rama to avenge Shurpanakha, and was destroyed by him alone.

cards.title: Maricha
cards.subtitle: The Golden Deer
cards.description: The shape-shifting rakshasa who, against his own warning, took the form of a golden deer to lure Rama away so that Ravana could seize Sita.

cards.title: The Golden Deer
cards.subtitle: A Fatal Lure
cards.description: Enchanted by a deer of gold, Sita begged Rama to capture it. The deer was Maricha in disguise, and the chase drew Rama far from the hut.

cards.title: The Lakshman Rekha
cards.subtitle: The Line Not to Cross
cards.description: The protective line Lakshmana is said to have drawn around the hut before leaving Sita. Tricked into stepping past it, she fell into Ravana's hands.

cards.title: Ravana
cards.subtitle: The Ten-Headed King
cards.description: The mighty rakshasa emperor of Lanka, a learned devotee of Shiva turned tyrant. His abduction of Sita brought the wrath of Rama upon him.

cards.title: The Ten Heads of Ravana
cards.subtitle: Knowledge and Pride
cards.description: Ravana's ten heads are said to signify his mastery of the six shastras and four Vedas — vast learning ruined by ungoverned pride.

cards.title: The Abduction of Sita
cards.subtitle: Ravana's Crime
cards.description: Disguised as a mendicant, Ravana carried Sita off in his flying chariot to Lanka, the act that set the whole war in motion.

cards.title: Jatayu's Last Stand
cards.subtitle: A Vulture's Sacrifice
cards.description: The aged vulture Jatayu fought Ravana in the sky to save Sita. Mortally wounded, he lived only long enough to tell Rama which way she was taken.

cards.title: Kabandha
cards.subtitle: The Headless Demon
cards.description: A cursed gandharva turned into a monstrous armed torso. Slain by Rama, he was freed from his curse and pointed the brothers toward Sugriva.

cards.title: Shabari
cards.subtitle: The Devoted Ascetic
cards.description: The aged tribal devotee who waited a lifetime to receive Rama, offering him berries she had carefully tasted to ensure their sweetness.

cards.title: Pampa Lake
cards.subtitle: The Waters of Kishkindha
cards.description: The lotus-covered lake near Rishyamukha where Rama, grieving for Sita, first met Hanuman and was led to Sugriva.

cards.title: Hanuman
cards.subtitle: The Son of the Wind
cards.description: The mighty vanara, son of the wind-god and supreme devotee of Rama. His strength, wisdom, and devotion carry the heart of the epic's second half.

cards.title: Sugriva
cards.subtitle: The Exiled Vanara King
cards.description: The monkey king of Kishkindha, driven out by his brother Vali. Allied with Rama, he lent the vanara host to the search for Sita.

cards.title: Vali
cards.subtitle: The Mighty Vanara
cards.description: The immensely strong king of Kishkindha and brother of Sugriva, blessed to drain half the strength of any foe he faced. Rama slew him to right Sugriva's wrong.

cards.title: The Slaying of Vali
cards.subtitle: An Arrow from Hiding
cards.description: To restore Sugriva to his throne, Rama struck Vali with an arrow from concealment during their duel — a deed long debated for its dharma.

cards.title: Tara
cards.subtitle: The Wise Queen
cards.description: The clever and eloquent queen of Kishkindha, wife of Vali. Her counsel and her grief are among the most affecting passages of the epic.

cards.title: Angada
cards.subtitle: Vali's Valiant Son
cards.description: The young vanara prince, son of Vali, who became a chief commander in Rama's army and an envoy to Ravana's court.

cards.title: Jambavan
cards.subtitle: The King of Bears
cards.description: The ancient and wise lord of the bears who counselled the search party and reminded Hanuman of his own forgotten powers.

cards.title: Kishkindha
cards.subtitle: The Vanara Kingdom
cards.description: The mountain realm of the monkey-folk ruled by Sugriva, from which the great search for Sita was launched in all directions.

cards.title: The Vanara Sena
cards.subtitle: The Monkey Army
cards.description: The vast host of vanaras and bears who rallied to Rama, built the great bridge, and stormed Lanka in his cause.

cards.title: Sampati
cards.subtitle: Jatayu's Brother
cards.description: The wingless elder vulture who, learning of Jatayu's death, told the searchers that Sita was held across the sea in Lanka.

cards.title: Mount Mahendra
cards.subtitle: The Leaping Peak
cards.description: The great seaside mountain from which Hanuman gathered himself and sprang across the ocean toward Lanka.

cards.title: Hanuman's Leap
cards.subtitle: Across the Ocean
cards.description: Swelling to enormous size, Hanuman bounded a hundred yojanas over the sea to Lanka, overcoming trials of sea-demons along the way.

cards.title: Surasa
cards.subtitle: The Mother of Serpents
cards.description: A sea-demoness sent to test Hanuman during his leap. He passed through her jaws by shrinking small, proving cunning as well as strength.

cards.title: Lanka
cards.subtitle: Ravana's Golden City
cards.description: The fabled island-fortress of the rakshasas, gleaming with gold, ruled by Ravana and ringed by the sea.

cards.title: Ashoka Vatika
cards.subtitle: Sita's Captivity
cards.description: The grove of ashoka trees in Lanka where Sita was held captive, guarded by rakshasis, refusing all of Ravana's threats and blandishments.

cards.title: Trijata
cards.subtitle: The Kindly Rakshasi
cards.description: The gentle demoness among Sita's guards who comforted her with a dream foretelling Ravana's fall and Rama's victory.

cards.title: Rama's Signet Ring
cards.subtitle: A Token of Truth
cards.description: The ring Hanuman carried to Sita as proof that he came from Rama, and which she answered with her own jewel.

cards.title: The Chudamani
cards.subtitle: Sita's Crest-Jewel
cards.description: The hair-jewel Sita entrusted to Hanuman to carry back to Rama, a token of her endurance and a sign she still lived.

cards.title: The Burning of Lanka
cards.subtitle: A Tail Aflame
cards.description: Captured and his tail set alight by the rakshasas, Hanuman leapt across the rooftops and set the golden city ablaze before bounding home.

cards.title: Vibhishana
cards.subtitle: The Righteous Brother
cards.description: The virtuous younger brother of Ravana who counselled returning Sita. Cast out, he joined Rama and was promised the throne of Lanka.

cards.title: Nala
cards.subtitle: The Bridge-Builder
cards.description: The vanara architect, son of the divine craftsman, who engineered the great causeway of floating stones across the sea to Lanka.

cards.title: Setu Bandha
cards.subtitle: Building the Bridge
cards.description: The vanara host bridged the ocean with boulders and tree-trunks, some said to float because the name of Rama was written upon them.

cards.title: Rama Setu
cards.subtitle: The Causeway to Lanka
cards.description: The long bridge of stones raised across the sea, over which Rama's army marched to lay siege to Lanka.

cards.title: The Rakshasas
cards.subtitle: The Hosts of Lanka
cards.description: The fierce demon-folk of Lanka, masters of illusion and war, who fought for Ravana against the vanara army.

cards.title: Kumbhakarna
cards.subtitle: The Sleeping Giant
cards.description: Ravana's colossal brother, cursed to sleep for months at a time. Woken to fight, he devoured warriors by the score before Rama felled him.

cards.title: Death of Kumbhakarna
cards.subtitle: The Mountain Falls
cards.description: Roused from his long sleep, the giant Kumbhakarna wrought havoc on the battlefield until Rama severed his great limbs and head with celestial arrows.

cards.title: Indrajit
cards.subtitle: The Conqueror of Indra
cards.description: Ravana's eldest son Meghnad, who once defeated Indra himself. A master of illusion and the deadly serpent-arrows, he was slain at last by Lakshmana.

cards.title: The Naga-Pasha
cards.subtitle: The Serpent Noose
cards.description: Indrajit's serpent-arrows that bound Rama and Lakshmana in living coils, until Garuda, foe of snakes, came and freed them.

cards.title: Garuda
cards.subtitle: The Eagle of Vishnu
cards.description: The divine eagle and mount of Vishnu, whose mere approach scattered Indrajit's serpent-bonds and revived the fallen brothers.

cards.title: The Sanjeevani
cards.subtitle: The Herb of Life
cards.description: The luminous life-restoring herb growing on a far Himalayan peak. Unable to find it, Hanuman carried back the whole mountain to heal Lakshmana.

cards.title: Hanuman and the Mountain
cards.subtitle: Lifting Dronagiri
cards.description: When Lakshmana lay dying, Hanuman flew to the Himalayas and, unable to pick the right herb, bore the entire glowing mountain back to the battlefield.

cards.title: The Fall of Indrajit
cards.subtitle: Lakshmana Prevails
cards.description: After fierce illusory battles, Lakshmana slew the sorcerer-prince Indrajit, breaking the back of Lanka's resistance.

cards.title: Mandodari
cards.subtitle: Ravana's Queen
cards.description: The wise and virtuous chief queen of Ravana, who pleaded with him in vain to return Sita and avoid the ruin of Lanka.

cards.title: Prahasta
cards.subtitle: Ravana's Commander
cards.description: The veteran general and chief minister of Ravana, commander of the Lankan armies, who fell to the vanara host before the city walls.

cards.title: The Pushpaka Vimana
cards.subtitle: The Flying Chariot
cards.description: The self-moving aerial chariot seized by Ravana, which later bore Rama, Sita, and their companions home to Ayodhya through the sky.

cards.title: The Brahmastra
cards.subtitle: The Ultimate Weapon
cards.description: The irresistible divine missile invoked with sacred mantras. Rama loosed it to end Ravana, striking the demon-king through the heart.

cards.title: The Slaying of Ravana
cards.subtitle: The Demon-King's End
cards.description: In their final duel Rama, counselled in the secret of Ravana's life, pierced him with the Brahmastra and freed the world of his tyranny.

cards.title: The Agni Pariksha
cards.subtitle: The Trial by Fire
cards.description: To answer doubts of her purity after captivity, Sita entered fire; the god Agni bore her out unharmed, affirming her unstained virtue.

cards.title: Sita's Fidelity
cards.subtitle: The Devoted Wife
cards.description: Sita's unwavering faithfulness through captivity and trial, held up by the epic as the ideal of the pativrata, the devoted wife.

cards.title: Return to Ayodhya
cards.subtitle: The First Diwali
cards.description: The exiles' homecoming after fourteen years, when the people lit rows of lamps to welcome Rama — remembered ever after as Diwali.

cards.title: The Sarayu
cards.subtitle: River of Ayodhya
cards.description: The sacred river flowing past Ayodhya, witness to Rama's birth, his departure, his return, and at the last his passing from the world.

cards.title: Ayodhya
cards.subtitle: Rama's Capital
cards.description: The ancient capital of Kosala on the Sarayu, seat of the Ikshvaku dynasty and the city to which Rama returned in triumph.

cards.title: Coronation of Rama
cards.subtitle: The Crown at Last
cards.description: His exile fulfilled, Rama was crowned king of Ayodhya amid universal rejoicing, beginning the reign remembered as the golden age.

cards.title: Rama Rajya
cards.subtitle: The Ideal Kingdom
cards.description: The legendary reign of Rama, a byword for perfect, just, and prosperous rule in which dharma flourished and all suffering ceased.

cards.title: Bhakti
cards.subtitle: Hanuman's Devotion
cards.description: The path of loving devotion embodied above all by Hanuman, whose selfless service to Rama became its eternal model.

cards.title: Lava
cards.subtitle: Rama's Son
cards.description: One of the twin sons of Rama and Sita, born in Valmiki's hermitage and taught the Ramayana, which he sang before his unknowing father.

cards.title: Kusha
cards.subtitle: Rama's Son
cards.description: The other twin of Rama and Sita, raised by Valmiki and skilled in arms and song, who with his brother recited the epic of their own father.
